$comment: Celeborn GitHub App manifest. Feed this to GitHub's 'Create a GitHub App from a manifest' flow (https://docs.github.com/apps/sharing-github-apps/registering-a-github-app-from-a-manifest). LEAST PRIVILEGE: the only WRITE scope is `checks` — there is NO contents/issues/pull_requests write, which makes pushing memory back out to a PR/issue structurally impossible (the no-Memory-on-PR guarantee). Replace every <your-project> placeholder with your Supabase project ref before submitting. See references/github-app-listing.md for the Marketplace copy and the publisher-verification note.
name: Celeborn Memory
description: Celeborn watches your repo's committed memory (.context/) for drift and captures PR/issue threads for your team's local context. It NEVER writes to your code, PRs, or issues — its only write is a neutral CI check.
